title: Tool use permission and training rule
summary: Practical guidance for “Tool use permission and training rule” within “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”. It supports clear responsibilities, verified information, visible records, fair priorities, stop conditions, and a workable handover, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.
content:
Practical guidance for “Tool use permission and training rule” within “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”. It supports clear responsibilities, verified information, visible records, fair priorities, stop conditions, and a workable handover, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.

Start with: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Tool use permission and training rule” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.

Stop condition: Do not work on live mains electricity, gas systems, pressurised vessels, damaged lithium batteries, or unstable structures without qualified help. Apply this boundary to “Tool use permission and training rule” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.

Reduced fallback: If “Tool use permission and training rule” cannot be completed, do only the first verified step, mark the remaining capability unavailable, and use a smaller documented task with one owner, one deadline, and one independent check.
materials: load and battery inventory — for “Tool use permission and training rule” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”
insulated dry storage — for “Tool use permission and training rule” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”

steps: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Tool use permission and training rule” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.

Define the essential function, available energy, rated limits, hazards, and a manual fallback before connecting equipment. Record the result under “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience” before continuing “Tool use permission and training rule”.

Inspect plugs, insulation, batteries, fuel, ventilation, moving parts, guards, moisture, and structural support. Recheck this point before closing “Tool use permission and training rule” for “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.

Disconnect or isolate the source before adjustment; use the correct tool, rating, polarity, protection, and dry working area. Treat any uncertainty here as an unresolved item in “Tool use permission and training rule” for “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.
Test one small reversible step under observation before depending on the repair or power arrangement. A second person should be able to verify this part of “Tool use permission and training rule”.

Label status, limits, last test, remaining capacity, fault signs, and the next inspection. Include the outcome of “Tool use permission and training rule” in the handover for “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.
warnings: Do not work on live mains electricity, gas systems, pressurised vessels, damaged lithium batteries, or unstable structures without qualified help. Apply this boundary to “Tool use permission and training rule” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.

Stop immediately for heat, swelling, smoke, sparks, burning smell, fuel leak, unusual noise, vibration, or repeated protective-device trips. Apply this boundary to “Tool use permission and training rule” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.
common_mistakes: Skipping the opening control makes “Tool use permission and training rule” unreliable: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. Apply this boundary to “Tool use permission and training rule” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.

Treating the result as safe while ignoring this limit is a serious error: Do not work on live mains electricity, gas systems, pressurised vessels, damaged lithium batteries, or unstable structures without qualified help. Apply this boundary to “Tool use permission and training rule” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.

Failing to document the final handover action leaves the next person without a trustworthy starting point: Label status, limits, last test, remaining capacity, fault signs, and the next inspection. Apply this boundary to “Tool use permission and training rule” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.
alternatives: If “Tool use permission and training rule” cannot be completed, do only the first verified step, mark the remaining capability unavailable, and use a smaller documented task with one owner, one deadline, and one independent check.
Use a dated paper note and spoken read-back for “Tool use permission and training rule” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience” when the normal form, device, or communication channel is unavailable.
failure_signs: Stop and reassess “Tool use permission and training rule” when the repair exceeds its rating, heats, loosens, leaks, trips protection, or changes behaviour under load. Apply this boundary to “Tool use permission and training rule” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.

Stop and reassess “Tool use permission and training rule” when the manual fallback is unavailable or the fault cannot be isolated and explained. Apply this boundary to “Tool use permission and training rule” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue “Tool use permission and training rule” while the load, stored energy, structural stability, material identity, ventilation, or isolation from electricity, pressure, heat, fuel, and moving parts is uncertain. Apply this boundary to “Tool use permission and training rule” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.

This card is not authorization to work on live utilities, support people under an improvised load, mix unknown chemicals, or treat a temporary repair as permanent. Topic-specific stop check for “Tool use permission and training rule”: also follow this warning: “Do not work on live mains electricity, gas systems, pressurised vessels, damaged lithium batteries, or unstable structures without qualified help in “Tool use permission and training rule”.”. Apply this boundary to “Tool use permission and training rule” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.
short_term: Complete one verified cycle for “Tool use permission and training rule” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”, record its owner, time, result, unresolved risk, and next review.
long_term: Review “Tool use permission and training rule” after real use in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”; replace failed materials, practise the handover, and update thresholds when official guidance or local conditions change.
if_method_fails: If the method described in “Tool use permission and training rule” gives no verifiable result, stop relying on it, isolate the uncertain output, record where it failed, and switch to a smaller documented task with one owner, one deadline, and one independent check.
environment_notes: Adapt the guidance in “Tool use permission and training rule” for “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience” to weather, lighting, crowding, accessibility, power, water, transport, and available skill without removing the stop condition or independent check.
